steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts how the volcanoes look similar from afar with the fact that volcanologists can tell them apart. The blank must express a concessive relationship — 'despite the appearance, the reality is different.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — in addition,: Adds information rather than acknowledging a contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — therefore,: Implies the second fact follows causally from the first, but the sentences are in tension, not cause-and-effect. ✗
  • Choice C — for example,: Introduces an illustration of a prior claim; that's not what the second sentence does. ✗
  • Choice D — though,: Means 'even though' or 'despite' — it acknowledges the surface similarity while introducing the deeper truth that volcanologists can distinguish the types. ✓
That is the correct answer.
